steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank precedes an appositive phrase — "the cellular organs that generate energy from light" — that renames the noun before it. After the appositive, an em-dash appears before "reshuffled." The question tests punctuation consistency around an appositive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — em-dash before the appositive: the appositive ends with an em-dash, so it must open with one. Matching em-dashes correctly set off the appositive on both sides. ✓
  • Choice B — comma before the appositive: mixing a comma opener with an em-dash closer is inconsistent punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — semicolon before the appositive: semicolons separate independent clauses, not appositives. ✗
  • Choice D — no punctuation before the appositive: leaves the appositive unset off, violating the rule that requires matching punctuation on both sides. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
